title: The First House
quote:
My baptism and first communion were not my decisions; they were my mother
replicating the schema she herself had received.
scene:
The very first ground: four years in Villeurbanne before the move. This
location also hosts The Font — a baptism, not chosen. A mother
replicating the schema she herself received.
concept_link:
The Initial Point of the Behavioral Chain; the first lesson in how tribes
transmit themselves — not through argument, but through repetition.

title: The Quiet Street
concepts: The Tribe (unspoken rules, fear of judgment/exclusion)
quote:
The most powerful tribes are not the ones that threaten exclusion openly, but
the ones whose rules are so deeply woven into daily life that they never need
to be spoken.
scene:
An ordinary residential neighborhood where nothing dramatic ever happens —
and that is precisely the point. The most powerful tribes are the ones whose
rules are woven so deep into daily life they never need to be spoken.
concept_link: The Tribe: benevolence installs programs just as firmly as cruelty does.

title: Venus's Garden
quote:
Growing up surrounded by animals installed a value so deep I never noticed
it being installed: animal life counts.
scene:
A black-and-white female Border Collie, born the same year as the author;
they grow up in parallel. Vega the cat joins at seven, then three more cats.
Venus dies at fourteen — and in a house without vocabulary for feelings,
that grief is one of the few memories where the author can still feel the
weight of what he felt.
concept_link:
The Tribe's non-human members; the value "animal life counts" installed so
deep it was never noticed being installed. Start of the inherited-line
thread (→ The Dashboard: conscious extension into veganism).

title: The Locked Room
concepts: Behavioral Chain (full): Initial Point → Values → Programs → Behaviours → Displays
The Beast vs the Cartographer (which cannot map what the Tribe forbade it to visit)
quote:
Mine taught me to value education, to distrust dogma, to work hard, to extend
compassion beyond my own species — and to keep my emotions locked in a room I
was never given the key to.
scene:
The family living room: affection exists but is never displayed. No hugs, no
"I love you," no vocabulary for feelings. Somewhere in the house, a room the
author was never given the key to.
concept_link:
The full Behavioral Chain: undemonstrative household (Initial Point) →
"emotions are private" (Value) → "do not express, do not name" (Program) →
no words, no tears (Behaviours) → friendships that dissolve (Displays). And
the lock holds from the other side too: the Cartographer cannot map territory
the Tribe taught it never to visit.

title: The Displayed Diplomas
quote:
My tribe never told me "you must succeed academically"; it simply displayed
success in a way my young brain recorded as the standard.
scene:
Family gatherings where uncles and aunts from prestigious engineering schools
simply exist. No one says "you must succeed academically"; success is
displayed, and a young brain records it as the standard. The father, a
printer by inherited vocation, shows little interest in grades.
concept_link:
Values installed by display, not command — the Tribe → Values link of the
Behavioral Chain.

title: Grandmother's Table
concepts: The Beast (cannot be reasoned with, only negotiated with)
quote: Some programs were installed by force rather than silence.
scene:
Entire afternoons seated before plates of spinach and cabbage. Her trigger
was care; the Beast recorded only coercion. Decades later, the limbic system
still vetoes those vegetables before the Cartographer can open the discussion.
concept_link:
A small but perfect illustration that the Beast cannot be reasoned with,
only negotiated with.

title: The Aquarium
quote: The aquarium was my first laboratory.
scene:
Early adolescence: a large aquarium in the living room, earned through trust.
To deserve that trust, the author devours books on fishkeeping — teaching
himself an entire ecosystem's rules before touching it. The first laboratory.
concept_link:
The first clear display of Play/Learn, the motivation that will dominate the
hierarchy — and the Coachman's method in embryo: rather than fighting a
system, study its rules and adjust its parameters. Start of the
play-learn-line thread (→ Control Panel → B&W Phone → Mont Blanc cockpit).

title: The Corner
scene:
Age sixteen, a countryside village. After a moment of intimacy with a friend
whose relationship was falling apart, her boyfriend — older, trained in
combat sports — corners the author and beats him while he cannot defend
himself. The bruises faded; the cracked belief did not: "my safe, benevolent
world will always protect me."
concept_link:
The early Impact Point. The Beast learns that danger is real and the body
defenseless — feeding the freeze response and orienting the Values Compass
"Away": away from confrontation, exposure, judgment.

title: The Van
quote: But it was on the road itself that the program revealed itself most clearly.
scene:
Between the end of the bachelor's degree and the start of engineering school:
six months alone in a van across New Zealand. Against all expectation, the
tribe never judged the choice — because it clashed with no value it had
transmitted. Neither the calling nor the academic trajectory was being
questioned, only postponed.
concept_link:
A boundary of the Tribe's authority: permission is granted freely where no
transmitted value is threatened. The Tribe polices its programs, not your
distance from it.

title: The Long Solo Hikes
concepts: 6 Motivations (Play/Learn + Survival at full strength, Tribe at zero)
scene:
What marked him was not, first and foremost, the encounters — intense as they
were — but the long solo hikes: entire days with nothing but the terrain, the
weather, and his own body to tame.
concept_link:
The motivational hierarchy seen in isolation: Play/Learn and Survival running
at full strength with Tribe at zero, and complete contentment. The clearest
natural experiment in the whole essay.

title: The Closed Bond
place: South Island — crossed almost end to end
concepts: Behavioral Chain (reverting to its original pattern)
scene:
He crossed nearly the whole of the South Island alongside a friend met on the
road — a bond as immediate and strong as only travel encounters produce. They
have seen each other once since, in France. He knows today that this
relationship is closed.
concept_link:
The Behavioral Chain reverting to its original pattern: the program installed
by an undemonstrative household — connection exists or it dissolves, it is
not maintained — held just as true on the other side of the world. It simply
redeployed itself onto new terrain.

title: The Return
concepts: The Tribe (activates only for old bonds)
scene:
He saw the few friends he had left as if nothing had happened — without
awkwardness, without any need to explain himself, because they already knew
him.
concept_link:
The clearest proof of the program: the relationship to the Tribe activates
only for old bonds, never for those that would need to be built from scratch.

title: The Control Panel
quote:
If "Who I Was" describes the programs my tribe installed, "Who I Am"
describes the moment I began reading my own code.
scene:
Every setting of the washing machine, configured before launch. Buttons
pressed for the pleasure of pressing them; boredom the moment anything
becomes familiar. The adolescent of the aquarium has simply changed scale.
concept_link:
Play/Learn first among the 6 Motivations: the brain rewards novelty and
mastery of systems far more than status or money.

title: The Dashboard
concepts: Behavioral Chain: conscious extension of an inherited value
scene:
Sleep data, cardiac data, ingredient lists — knowing exactly what enters the
body. Veganism sits here at the intersection of two chains: the ethical
extension of the tribe's love of animals, and survival-by-optimization. One
discipline, two justifications.
concept_link:
Survival, second motivation, in its modern form — the deliberate mirror of
Grandmother's Table: where her generation survived by force-feeding, this one
survives by optimization, coercion replaced with informed consent. End point
of the inherited-line thread: the value "animal life counts," inherited
from a meat-eating tribe and consciously extended further than they took it.

title: The Grey Wardrobe / Behind the Wall
quote:
My current work is to close it from the inside out — starting, as the course
suggests, by convincing my own mind of confidence first, in order to bring the
Beast along rather than fight it.
scene:
Outside: deliberately unremarkable clothes, a body language that reads cold
or nonchalant, a wish at times to be invisible, to disturb no one. Inside:
a mind hungry for novelty, an ethics strong enough to restructure a diet,
a person open and unrestrained once trust exists.
concept_link:
The Profiling Matrix — the gap between Visible and Non-Visible is the
shame-triggered Beast building a wall, and strangers mistake the wall for
the person. Exactly what a profiler would read.

title: The Black-and-White Phone
concepts: Habit Loop: Trigger unchangeable → Action modifiable → Reward unchangeable
quote:
I no longer try to defeat the Beast in a direct fight, because I have
understood that this fight cannot be won in 0.1 seconds.
scene:
Boredom, the unchangeable trigger; dopamine, the unchangeable reward.
Deleting the networks failed — too many people live on Instagram. So the
environment is engineered instead: an automation drains the iPhone of color
the instant Instagram opens, stripping the reward of its visual food.
concept_link:
The Habit Loop with the Action re-engineered — the Coachman redesigning the
route rather than whipping the horses. In miniature, the aquarium method:
study a system's rules and adjust its parameters.

title: The Crossroads of Impulses
concepts: 6 Motivations: the unnamed need for connection
quote:
I did not choose to devalue the tribe; my tribe, through its silence, taught
me to.
scene:
The urge to contact an ex, redirected toward calling a friend: the same
underlying need for connection, a healthier target.
concept_link:
Distracting the Beast — one of the three strategies (let it happen /
discuss / distract). Also evidence that Tribe, ranked last in the
motivational hierarchy, still runs underneath: the need for connection
exists, merely running under an old program that refuses to name it.

title: The Pushback Bench
quote:
Who I am, then, is a person in mid-negotiation with his own programming:
still wired for invisibility and control, but now armed with a map of the
machine — and beginning, deliberately, to rewire it.
scene:
Someone advises or asks; the reflex is to push back and contradict — an
unconscious loop likely tied to a need for control. The strategy borrows
from Stoicism: letting go of what cannot be controlled. It is difficult,
and the loop still wins regularly.
concept_link:
An unresolved Habit Loop; naming it is the Cartographer's first act of
reclaiming territory from the Beast.

title: The Lake — September 2026
quote:
Play/Learn drives all of them — a triathlon, a boat, a plane: new systems to
master, new buttons to press, on an ever-larger scale.
scene:
September 2026, AS IF: the boat license is held. Autonomous navigation,
days on the water with friends.
concept_link:
The AS IF mindset — goals written as accomplished facts, the Cartographer
drawing the map before the territory exists, so the Beast learns to treat
it as home. Play/Learn made concrete: a new system to master.

title: The Improv Stage — January 2027
concepts: Beast strategy: discuss / training the Beast
quote:
The final section of this essay is written, deliberately, in the "AS IF"
mindset — not as a wish list, but as a description of an identity already in
motion.
scene:
January 2027, AS IF: enrolled in improvisation classes — the most direct
assault on the old programming. The unscripted reply, the eyes of strangers:
the exact terrain where the Beast has always failed, chosen because of the
shame trigger, not despite it.
concept_link:
Not fighting the Beast — training it, discussion by discussion, until
unexpected situations stop registering as threats. Narrative climax of
Zone C.

title: The Finish Line — September 2028
concepts: Habit Loops in service of goals
scene:
September 2028, AS IF: a confirmed triathlete crosses an Olympic-distance
finish line — mountains rising behind blue water — after four years of
step-by-step progress in each discipline, and the Beast, once fed this
reward, already wants the Ironman.
concept_link:
Habit architecture in service of goals; Play/Learn and Survival driving
endurance. The Beast's reward system turned ally.

title: The Signed Keys — September 2028
concepts: 6 Motivations: Survival (security); Recognition and Reward notably absent
quote:
Survival drives the discipline of endurance sport and the security of
property ownership.
scene:
September 2028, AS IF: the engineering degree completed, a permanent
contract signed, and the largest loan the bank will grant — a home.
concept_link:
The motivational hierarchy made concrete. Notably absent: goals of pure
Recognition or Reward — no audience chased, no fortune. The goals are more
honest for it.

title: The Idea Log
concepts: Entrepreneurship as a system learned patiently
scene:
A long-term brainstorming log, noting every idea however insignificant —
entrepreneurship treated as a system to be learned patiently rather than a
lightning strike to be waited for.
concept_link:
Play/Learn applied to the professional future; the aquarium method at the
scale of a career.

title: The Future Team
quote:
Emotions — the pillar my tribe never taught me — will remain my hardest
apprenticeship: one cannot move people through emotions one struggles to name
in oneself.
scene:
Leadership as influence — mutual benefit — never manipulation. Two pillars
already stand: Reciprocity (giving consistently, expecting the same) and
Credibility (technical choices justified daily, influence earned through
demonstrated competence). Authority will come with status and experience.
Two remain under construction: Emotions — the pillar the tribe never
taught — and connecting to others' Motivations and Values, which requires
loosening control. The belief "you are never better served than by
yourself" sits here, named as a program, awaiting its impact point.
Perhaps the future team will provide it.
concept_link:
The 5 pillars of influence; a belief in tension with leadership itself —
the art of being served through others by serving them first.

title: Above Mont Blanc — 2029
quote:
Who I will be, then, is not a different person — it is the same machine,
deliberately reprogrammed: a Beast trained rather than feared, a Coachman who
redesigns routes instead of whipping horses, and a compass finally pointing
Toward.
scene:
Early 2029: registered for the private pilot license — soon flying
autonomously, leaving for weekends, passing over Mont Blanc.
concept_link:
The literal inversion of invisibility: rising into full view, gaze assumed.
The Values Compass fully reoriented Toward; the end of the play-learn-line
thread — cockpit buttons at the scale of the sky.
